sentences.english: My first part-time job was washing dishes in a restaurant. It wasn't a very glamorous job, but I really wanted it because I wanted to pay for horse-riding lessons.
sentences.korean: 첫 아르바이트는 식당에서 설거지하는 거였어요. 화려한 일은 아니었지만, 겨우 13살이라 내 돈을 벌고 싶어서 정말 하고 싶었어요.

sentences.english: So every Saturday night I went to this restaurant and worked for maybe five hours. It was hard physical labor, but it taught me the value of money.
sentences.korean: 매주 토요일 밤에 이 식당에 가서 약 5시간 일했어요. 힘든 육체노동이었지만 노력에 대해 많이 배웠어요.

sentences.english: My first part-time job — I was living in a small town, Tulsa, Oklahoma, and I worked for the Harmon Foundation. They owned a hands-on science and math museum for children.
sentences.korean: 첫 아르바이트 — 오클라호마 털사라는 작은 마을에 살면서 하몬 재단에서 일했어요. 거기서 어린이를 위한 체험형 과학·수학 박물관을 운영했어요.

sentences.english: Every day I would run a laser light show, and once I was through with that I would give museum tours and explain the science and math background to each exhibit.
sentences.korean: 매일 레이저 라이트 쇼를 진행하고, 끝나면 박물관 투어를 하면서 모든 전시물의 과학과 수학을 설명했어요.

sentences.english: It was a lot of fun seeing kids open their eyes to mathematics and science.
sentences.korean: 아이들이 수학과 과학에 눈을 뜨는 걸 보는 게 정말 재미있었어요.

quiz.question: What does '남은 음식/물건' mean in English?

sentences.english: My first part-time job was as a cashier at a supermarket.
I really hated it because I had to stand on my feet for basically eight hours a day, dealing with a lot of people who don't appreciate the job that we do.
sentences.korean: 정말 싫었어요. 기본적으로 하루 8시간 서 있어야 하고, 인사도 안 하고 고맙다는 말도 없는 사람들을 많이 상대해야 했거든요.

sentences.english: I had a lot of people yelling at me for different reasons and always misunderstanding the situations. It's not a job I would go back to.
sentences.korean: 여러 이유로 소리 지르는 사람들이 많았고 항상 상황을 오해했어요. 추천하고 싶지 않은 일이에요.

sentences.english: My first part-time job was working at McDonald's. When I was fifteen, I had a job at McDonald's flipping burgers, doing fries, cooking — not taking orders.
sentences.korean: 첫 아르바이트는 맥도날드에서 일하는 거였어요. 15살 때 맥도날드에서 버거를 뒤집고, 감자튀김을 만들고, 손님을 응대했어요.

sentences.english: I enjoyed it for a while, about two or three months, but then I started losing interest in it, and after six months, I quit the job. But it was a good job for a fifteen-year-old.
sentences.korean: 2~3개월 정도는 즐겼는데, 그 후에 흥미를 잃기 시작해서 6개월 후에 그만뒀어요.

quiz.question: What does '직접 체험하는' mean in English?

sentences.english: My first part-time job was at a bakery, in New Zealand.
I was in high school then, so about two times a week — the weekends and Fridays after school — I would go to the bakery and sell bread to the customers.
sentences.korean: 그때 고등학생이라 일주일에 두 번 정도 — 주말과 금요일 방과 후에 — 빵집에 가서 바닥 쓸기, 반죽 준비, 손님 응대 같은 걸 했어요.

sentences.english: The best part was that at the end of the day, all the leftover bread, we could take home to our families, which made my brother very, very happy.
sentences.korean: 가장 좋았던 건 하루가 끝나면 남은 빵을 모두 집에 가져갈 수 있었다는 거예요. 엄마가 좋아하셨어요.

sentences.english: My first part-time job was at a restaurant called Chuck E. Cheese. It's a famous restaurant in America for little kids. Sometimes I was a waiter, but sometimes I got to dress up as Chucky.
Chucky is a big mouse — he's the character for Chuck E. Cheese — and so I would dress up in a big mouse costume and go around entertaining little kids.
sentences.korean: 처키는 큰 쥐로 척 이 치즈의 캐릭터예요. 그래서 큰 쥐 의상을 입고 돌아다니면서 아이들과 놀았어요.

quiz.question: What does '직접 체험하는' mean in English?
